name: Khalil Center
mission: Our wellness centers integrate Islamic spiritual approaches into professional psychology to help achieve optimal, holistic health by providing a wide variety of mental health services.
theoryOfChange: Khalil Center utilizes the Traditional Islamically Integrated Psychotherapy (TIIP) framework, which is a therapeutic model developed by their leadership. This framework integrates professional psychology into an Islamic context, outlining Islamic beliefs regarding health, pathology, human ontology, and epistemology. It provides a refined integrative approach within an Islamic framework for psychotherapeutic practice and guides their services and research.
scoreSummary: Khalil Foundation shows good alignment and good impact, with zakat compliance (minor risk deductions applied).

evaluationSources: Practice-based evidence study published in 'Spirituality in Clinical Practice' (2023)
Research published in the Journal of Muslim Mental Health

dimension_explanations.alignment: The organization shows medium alignment with donor needs by addressing global health through a culturally specific lens, maintaining a clear track record in the Muslim community [2][6]. Its mission focuses on clinical mental health services and community education to bridge the gap between faith and psychology [4].
dimension_explanations.impact: The foundation demonstrates program effectiveness through the delivery of thousands of psychotherapy sessions and the training of professionals in TIIP [4][5]. While the program expense ratio is solid at 71.2%, the cost per beneficiary is approximately $907.87, reflecting the intensive nature of clinical capacity building [1][3].

baseline_narrative.headline: The Khalil Foundation integrates traditional Islamic principles with modern psychology to provide specialized clinical mental health services and community education.
strengths: High fundraising efficiency with only $0.01 spent to raise every $1
Strong program expense ratio of 71.2% dedicated to clinical and educational services
Clear zakat policy and eligibility for Muslim donors
baseline_narrative.summary: The Khalil Foundation, operating as the Khalil Center, provides clinical mental health services and psychotherapy sessions rooted in Traditional Islamically Integrated Psychotherapy (TIIP) [4][5]. The organization is recognized as zakat-eligible, utilizing funds to support eligible beneficiaries in need of psychological care [2].

impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that the integration of faith and psychology produces measurable clinical improvements for patients.

strengths.detail: The organization demonstrates remarkable efficiency in its development efforts, spending just <cite id="9">$0.01 for every $1 raised</cite>.

The center has achieved significant reach in a niche field, delivering over <cite id="6">70,000 lifetime psychotherapy sessions</cite> and training <cite id="7">500 professionals</cite> in its proprietary TIIP framework.
A high proportion of resources is directed toward its mission, with a <cite id="10">71.2% program expense ratio</cite> dedicated to clinical and educational services.

strengths_deep_dive: The Khalil Center's primary strength lies in its dual-track approach of direct service and systemic capacity building. By providing <cite id="5">13,065 clinical appointments</cite> annually, they address immediate community crises, while their <cite id="3">School of Islamic Psychology & Research</cite> ensures the long-term sustainability of culturally competent care. Their financial model is supported by a mix of <cite id="12">$796,691 in contributions</cite> and <cite id="13">$1,476,207 in program service revenue</cite>, indicating a sustainable social enterprise model that reduces total reliance on philanthropy.
rich_narrative.summary: The Khalil Foundation, operating as the Khalil Center, is a specialized psychological and spiritual wellness center that integrates <cite id="1">traditional Islamic principles with modern psychology</cite>. Founded in <cite id="2">2010</cite>, the organization addresses the unique mental health needs of the Muslim community through its <cite id="3">Traditional Islamically Integrated Psychotherapy (TIIP)</cite> framework. The center operates at a significant scale for a specialized provider, reporting <cite id="4">$2,272,898 in total revenue</cite> for the 2023 tax year. Their impact is evidenced by a high volume of direct service delivery, including <cite id="5">13,065 clinical appointments</cite> reported in a single year and a lifetime total of <cite id="6">70,136 psychotherapy sessions delivered</cite>. Beyond individual clinical care, the foundation serves as an educational hub, having <cite id="7">trained 500 professionals in the TIIP model</cite> and hosted <cite id="8">52 support groups</cite> to foster community-based healing. The organization maintains a lean administrative profile, spending only <cite id="9">$0.01 to raise every $1</cite> in contributions. While its <cite id="10">program expense ratio of 71.2%</cite> is strong, the specialized nature of clinical care results in a <cite id="11">cost per beneficiary of approximately $907.87</cite>, reflecting the intensive resource requirements of professional mental health services.
